hypothesis: Fixing three evidence-backed defects found by independent judges against Loop 01 (skip-link not moving focus into the wizard, Continue advancing past invalid empty steps, and Clear saved progress discarding state without confirmation) will resolve those specific navigation, error-prevention, and destructive-action-safety issues while preserving every frozen invariant and all previously passing objective gates.
interaction_paradigm: Unchanged from Loop 01: a true step-by-step wizard: exactly one primary task step (Contact, Delivery address, Payment, Review & place order) is the active/frontier step at a time, with explicit named progress ("Step N of 4: <name>"), a single active keyboard-safe Continue control per frontier step, Back and Edit controls to return to any completed step, and resumable/safe local save state. Steps not yet reached remain in the DOM but are hidden accessibly (aria-hidden, removed from tab order, visually collapsed) until the wizard journey reaches them; once a step is reached it stays reachable/editable. This Loop does not change the paradigm; it only fixes three specific defects within it.

human_use.rationale: The Artifact is a human-operated mobile checkout. Its success depends on discoverability, navigation, input burden, error recovery, feedback, accessibility, touch ergonomics, interruption recovery, perceived latency, destructive-action safety, and cognitive load across cross-disability use.

qualitative_lenses_note: These lenses are qualitative only in this metadata and in self-observation.md; no objective ergonomics claims are derived from them. Only the frozen harness's pass/fail assertions and metrics below are treated as objective evidence. No independent judge was run by this Loop.

scratch_root_activity.command_provenance_note: The exact shell invocations were omitted by the generator and cannot be recovered truthfully. Surviving scripts, outputs, timestamps, hashes, self-observation references, and the terminal task report establish that local Playwright probes ran.

input_feedback.full_verbatim_quotes_and_attribution: See prompt-history.md section 2 for the byte-for-byte verbatim evidence[].verbatim strings, sources, and attributions.

commands.note: First run after implementing all three evidence-backed changes. All 7 gates passed; blocking_failure=false. Non-gating metrics identical to Loop 01 (activations=24, completion_interactions=1, corrections=4, focusable_controls_traversed=29), which prompted a direct Playwright investigation (in generated/harness/scratch/) confirming each fix genuinely works for real user interaction, and that the unchanged metrics are explained by a pre-existing, Loop-01-inherited CSS characteristic (`.pending-control` max-height vs `.btn`/input min-height conflict, plus author-origin display:block overriding the UA `[hidden]` rule) that lets the harness's own fill()-based traversal reach later-step fields without needing Continue, independent of this Loop's changes. Preserved untouched in evidence-attempt-1/.
Final, required run using the exact --out path specified by the task. All 7 gates passed; blocking_failure=false; 287/287 assertions pass. This is the authoritative evidence directory for this Loop.

provenance_correction.reason: The terminal generator report contradicted the original scratch_root_not_written claim and disclosed deletion of unrelated root diagnostics after the procedural pause.

non_gating_task_efficiency_metrics_delta.note: Identical to Loop 01 on every non-gating metric. This is explained (see lesson and self-observation.md) by a pre-existing, unmodified-from-Loop-01 CSS rendering characteristic that lets the harness's own field-filling traversal reach later steps without needing to press Continue, independent of whether the new per-step validation-blocking logic is present. This unchanged count is not treated as evidence that the qualitative defects were not fixed: each of the three required fixes was independently verified with direct Playwright interaction probes simulating a real user (not the harness's own fill()-based traversal), confirming skip-link focus now lands on the step heading, Continue genuinely blocks and shows a step-scoped error summary on a blank step, and Clear saved progress requires an explicit confirm click before localStorage is touched.

lesson: All three evidence-backed defects reported against resumable-wizard-loop-01 were fixed and independently verified via direct Playwright interaction probes (not just the frozen harness's own traversal, which does not exercise all three paths): (1) skip-link activation now moves focus to the active step's heading instead of leaving it at BODY; (2) Continue now validates the active step and blocks/refocuses on error instead of always advancing, preventing errors from being distributed into hidden/revealed context; (3) Clear saved progress now requires an explicit confirm click (with Cancel as the safe default focus target and Escape support) before localStorage is discarded. A durable finding for future loops: the frozen harness's reveal()/fill_all() helpers rely on Playwright's is_visible(), which does not respect an ancestor's overflow:hidden clipping and does not treat a UA-origin [hidden] rule as authoritative once an author-origin rule (here, .pending-control's display:block, combined with .btn/input's min-height:44px outweighing max-height:0 per the CSS box-sizing conflict resolution) sets a conflicting display/size on the same element. This means the harness can read/fill later, not-yet-reached wizard steps without ever needing a Continue press, which is why adding real Continue-blocking validation produced no change in the harness's own non-gating interaction counts even though the fix is real and independently verified for actual user interaction (tabindex=-1 and aria-hidden correctly exclude these controls from keyboard/AT navigation, and their zero-height overflow:hidden ancestor gives a mouse user nothing on-screen to click). This CSS characteristic pre-dates this Loop (inherited unmodified from Loop 01) and was not corrected here, since it is outside the three required evidence-backed changes and touches shared sizing rules used across the whole candidate; it is proposed as a future Loop 03 follow-up only if further evidence supports it.
decision_note: All frozen objective gates pass with blocking_failure=false (287/287 assertions pass). No independent judge was run and no promotion/new_best/self-scoring claim is made, per task scope. This Loop's evidence is offered for independent human/judge review only, exactly as required.
